input.headline: ‘We could have asked ChatGPT’: students fight back over course taught by AI

raw.headline: ‘We could have asked ChatGPT’: students fight back over course taught by AI

winner: Students protested when their professor said he was teaching the class because "it's what I'm good at." Then they realized the real issue was that he wasn't actually using ChatGPT—he just used his own notes.

candidates.text: Why did the student bring a ladder to their AI-taught class? Because they heard the teacher was *raising* the bar—just like ChatGPT!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'raising the bar' and ChatGPT are clever and specific, with a natural flow and surprise factor."}

candidates.text: Why did the student bring a ladder to class? Because their professor said the education was too *elevated*—it was powered by ChatGPT!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'elevated' are clever and specific to the AI education context; concise and surprising with natural wordplay."}

candidates.text: The professor announced he'd be teaching a new course on "The Art of Humanity." Students were disappointed until they found out it was actually an AI. "Well," one said, "at least it won't grade on a curve.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with a specific scenario, concise and natural humor that plays on AI's lack of bias"}

candidates.text: Students protested when their professor said he was teaching the class because "it's what I'm good at." Then they realized the real issue was that he wasn't actually using ChatGPT—he just used his own notes.
